LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ENERGETIQ TECHNOLOGY, INC. MOTION FOR MENTAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF SONYA GARCIA IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 3, 2017, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO ALLOW DEFENDANTS TO CHANGE THE DESIGNATION OF THE MEDICAL PROVIDERS. THE COURT WILL ALLOW THE EXAMINATIONS TO OCCUR BUT WILL NOT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS BY CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTS THE PARTIES STIPULATION TO HAVE AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME FOR A MOTION TO COMPEL A FACT WITNESS DEPOSITION, WHICH SHALL BE HEARD ON APRIL 3, 2017, WITH THE PENDING MOTIONS. COUNSEL TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JOHN CAMERON, SONYA GARCIA, ANDRAE PETTIGRUE'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DISCOVERY CUTOFF TO CONDUCT DEPOSITION OF DEBBIE GUSTAFSON IS GRANTED. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT ENERGETIQ TECHNOLOGY, INC. MUST MAKE MS. GUSTAFSON AVAILABLE FOR A DEPOSITION OF NO MORE THAN THREE HOURS ON A DATE AGREEABLE TO ALL PARTIES NO LATER THAN APRIL 12, 2017. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO EXTEND THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF DATE AND TO TAKE MS. GUSTAFSON'S DEPOSITION. BECAUSE ENERGETIQ'S POSITION DID NOT LACK SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION, NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: KELLY DEYOUNG, CSR 6780 (KDEYOUNG@SFTC.ORG) (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Discovery Cutoff To Conduct Deposition Of Debbie Gustafson; Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS JOHN CAMERON, SONYA GARCIA, ANDRAE PETTIGRUE'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DISCOVERY CUTOFF TO CONDUCT DEPOSITION OF DEBBIE GUSTAFSON; REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS.

Appearances: Cassady Toles of Kern Noda Devine & Segal (415.474.1900) for defendant Michael McLaughlin; Steven Brady of the Brady Law Group (415.459.7300) for plaintiffs and responding parties; Justin F. Spearman of Bordin Martorell LLP (323.457.2110) for defendant Energetiq Technology, Inc.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Defendant Energetiq Technology, Inc. must make Ms. Gustafson available for a deposition of no more than three hours on a date agreeable to all parties no later than April 12, 2017. Plaintiffs have shown good cause to extend the discovery cutoff date and to take Ms. Gustafson's deposition. Because Energetiq's position did not lack substantial justification, no sanctions are awarded. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelly DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mental Examination Of Plaintiff John Cameron; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Joshua Bordin-Wosk
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Continued To Apr-03-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steven J. Brady, of Brady Law Group, Tel. 800.881.9011, for plaintiffs;
Justin F. Spearman, of Bordin Martorell LLP, Tel. 323.457.2110, for defendants Energetiq Technology, Inc.;
Cassady Toles, of Kern, Noda, Devine & Segal, Tel. 415.474.1900, for defendants Michael McLaughlin.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT ENERGETIQ TECHNOLOGY, INC. Motion For Mental Examination Of Plaintiff John Cameron is continued to April 3, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to allow defendants to change the designation of the medical providers. The Court will allow the examinations to occur but will not prejudice plaintiffs by continuing the trial date. The Court grants the parties stipulation to have an order shortening time for a Motion to Compel a Fact Witness Deposition, which shall be heard on April 3, 2017, with the pending motions. Counsel to prepare a proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
